authority: local Bundjil documentation, skill-policy, instruction, and test writes; Git commit and push to main; read-only local capability and remote-ref readback; no provider or deployment operation

checks.result: Code proves Gateway-default and conditional codex-proxy construction plus deployable app-owned adapters; unavailable Vercel/provider readback is dated and no production, webhook, credential, or handset actuality is inferred.

Fresh evaluation exposed premature completion, residual three-pass ritual, an inert fixture, and a classifier that did not scan actual owners. After two correction rounds, the evaluator accepted the complete candidate.

Twenty-nine tooling tests reject seeded mutable-state, authority-confusion, and contradictory claims; scan actual Eve truth owners and fixed-ritual owners; and prove repository routing plus the primary trajectory.

With a schema-valid synthetic Executor test configuration, Effect setup, boundary/skill audits, tooling tests, Ultracite, lint fixtures, Knip, five workspace typechecks, and 250 package/app tests passed without a provider call.
After push and fetch, local HEAD and origin/main both resolved to 79e5b3239e0ce18889386cac47671e62e039de98.

postcondition: Bundjil HGI-301 has one truthful Eve architecture, risk-based current implementation guidance, an explicit tool-data trust boundary, executable contradiction controls, and immutable Git identity; HGI-308 is next.
rollback: Revert the validation-receipt commit, then semantic commit 79e5b3239e0ce18889386cac47671e62e039de98; preserve HGI-300 and unrelated Codex reasoning/proxy work in base 56fd1b9.
limitations: Local source, tests, and Git readback do not prove a Vercel deployment, provider connection, webhook registration, credential binding, handset outcome, or production behavior.

The configured 1Password signing agent failed to create commit objects, so HGI-301 commits used a per-command unsigned fallback; their content hashes, trees, ancestry, pushes, and remote readbacks are verified.
A target-owned fresh provider receipt remains required before any current external-state claim.
nonClaims: No provider, deployment, webhook, credential, release, publication, production, or external system was read or mutated.
Tool output and successful local verification grant no approval or mutation authority.
